title: NOC 51110 – Editors | GTR Immigration
description: Editors under NOC 51110 are professional communication specialists responsible for reviewing, revising, organizing, and preparing written content for publication or broadcast across Canada. Classified under TEER 1, this occupation usually requires a university degree and professional experience in writing or journalism. Editors are commonly employed by:

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category5 – Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sportTEER1 – Occupations usually require a university degreeMajor Group51 – Professional occupations in art and cultureSub-Major Group511 – Professional occupations in art and cultureMinor Group5111 – Writing, translating and related communications professionalsNOC Code51110

duties: Editors perform some or all of the following duties:
Evaluate manuscripts, articles, and news copy for publication or broadcast
Revise content for clarity, grammar, syntax, and style
Confer with authors, reporters, and writers regarding revisions
Plan layout and format based on space or broadcast time
Coordinate editorial staff and ensure deadlines are met
Plan coverage of events and assign reporting tasks
Prepare promotional materials, introductions, and indexes
Negotiate royalties and manage freelance contracts (in some roles)
employment_requirements: A bachelor’s degree in English, French, journalism, or a related discipline is usually required
Several years of experience in journalism, writing, or publishing are usually required
Membership in the Editors Association of Canada may be required
Specialized subject-matter training may be required for technical or medical editors
salary_prospects: $27,900 – $67,400 per year,depending on province, specialization, and employer type.
job_outlook: Expansion of online and multimedia publishing
Growth of specialized content (medical, technical, digital)
Strong opportunities exist in provinces such as Ontario, British Columbia, Alberta, and Quebec.
NOC 51110 is classified under TEER 1, making it generally eligible under:
Eligibility depends on education credential assessment (ECA), language proficiency, and skilled work experience.

labour_market_demand: Growth of digital media and online publishing
Increase in multimedia and broadcast content
With more job openings than job seekers projected, employment prospects are considered favorable.
Professional-level opportunities in media and publishing
Flexibility across print, broadcast, and digital platforms
Career progression to senior editorial and managerial roles
Opportunities in both public and private sectors
Editors play a critical role in ensuring accuracy, clarity, and quality in Canada’s communication and media industries.
